'''''SOMETIMES EVIL LURKS IN THE PLACES YOU DONT EXPECT'''''
=='''Description'''== {{Blurimage|file=Screenshot 2025-02-25 104946.png|caption=A recorded instance of Public Execution. Page has been assumed to be ANNIHILATED}} '''Public Execution''' is a digital manifestation affecting pages within the [[The Database|Database]], mainly pages which were a byproduct of [[Temporary Manifestation|Phenomenon 99]] but can spread to other pages as well. '''Public Execution''' manifests as a bright red box on the top of a page, in a similar nature to the Database's delete, rewrite, and fix templates. However, unlike these templates, instances of '''Public Execution''' feature a bright red hexagonal logo, with the words "This page is a candidate for Public Execution" placed under it. Under these words are where the "reasons" for the page being deleted is located, which varies depending on the page. At the very bottom of each instance are the words "We recommend embracing for what's to come. God may be a merciful lord, we are not". As of now, these words are only assumed to be an empty threat and completely harmless, although more research is needed to prove this. Once an instance of '''Public Execution''' spawns on a page, it will stay up for a set amount of time, giving [[Backrooms Wiki:Staff|Database Moderators]] a chance to delete the page. During this timeframe, many database users will be lured into pages that possess an instance of Public Execution via anomalous means, and will proceed to post comments, giving more attention to the page and increasing the likelihood that it gets deleted by a moderator. If the page is not deleted within 35 minutes to an hour after spawn, the page will be deleted automatically, and the page's creator will be blocked for a varying amount of time, usually infinity, although this block is easily able to be counteracted by mods. It is unknown what exactly causes '''Public Execution''' to spawn, or why it targets certain types of pages, as the process is seemingly random and not based on any pattern other than '''Public Execution''' seemingly attacking byproduct pages of Phenomenon 99. It is currently assumed that an unknown AI algorithm is responsible for the phenomenon taking place, due to its effectiveness in deleting pages. Through this theory, an argument could be made that whatever AI program is responsible for '''Public Execution''' has not been developed fully yet, resulting in it accidentally sniping perfectly good pages. ==Addenda==
Reports on the nature of '''Public Execution''' As of recently, the properties of '''Public Execution''' has begun to change considerably. The threatening messages present within instances of '''Public Execution''' has ramped up, oftentimes appearing on the notice itself in the "reasons" section. Delete notices on pages deleted by '''Public Execution''' also began to glitch considerably, with properties such as showing distorted versions of moderator's names instead of just showing the word "executed" like normal, as well as showing dates and timeframes that do not exist. The threatening nature of '''Public Execution''' also found its way into the notices, with words such as "embrace death" and "I'm coming for you" being common. Alongside the properties listed above, disappearance cases involving members of the Database are on the rise, and each victim has been revealed to have their page targeted by '''Public Execution''' in the moments leading up to their disappearance. We are currently investigating these circumstances to determine if hhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhrvhekuarkaerurhvhnjekenrvrfnjljnvjbnjgrbkybgkbkjbmnhlnmhjygnykny-
Freedom is a wish and a blessing...
Harmful is an Afterthought...
At first, we assumed like everyone else would, that Public Execution was a harmless tool. One that we did not intend on having, but was a blessing.

It helped us get rid of trolls and vandals within the Database without any intervention. If an innocent user was blocked, we could just come in and unblock them, and life would go on as usual. It made life easier for us mods.

But sometimes, things are too good to be true.


{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
Someone was discovered in front of a BACK-NET terminal on [[Level 4]], dead. The cause of death was assumed to be blunt force trauma to the head.

Soon enough, a note was discovered next to the body, it read: "This fellow database user was executed for: "content was: {{Public Execution|1=YOU FUCKING SUCK. This page sucks and has loads of SPaG errors, the level doesnt even have the same number throughout, and it's so horribly written and SUCKS.
Rule 1.1 1.2 1.3 1.4 1.5 7.1 7.2 and A WHOLE LOTTA OTHER ONES!}}"

There's something more going on with Public Execution than we realize...

{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
[[File:Entitis.png|link=|780x780px|center]]
==
'''Ex'''
e
'''CUT'''
ed...
== Once a page is deleted by '''Public Execution''', any computer monitors prevalent nearby the page's creator will begin to glitch out and showcase a completely blank red screen within their displays. Shortly afterwards, an entity of unknown origin will emerge from one of the monitors, with the process itself described as "a macabre event where the red screen warps and stretches out of its digital prison and assumes a living form". If no computers or any digital device is nearby to the subject, entities will manifest once one arrives at a level containing computers. Immediately upon formation, instances will begin their relentless pursuit and gradually approach the subject, often using stealth tactics and moving quietly to avoid detection. '''Public Execution''' instances do not possess harm upon being spotted, however if any attempts are made to defeat the anomaly or blockade its pursuit, it will immediately become hostile. Once an instance approaches a subject, the entity will proceed to attack them, with attacks often fatal. Once the target is executed, instances will draw a message, mainly on paper and walls, explaining why the subject was killed. Shortly afterwards, it will transmit back into the nearest computer screen, where the instance supposedly manifests as another instance of the '''Public Execution''' notice. Once one is murdered by '''Public Execution''', their profile on the Database will be "blocked" for an indefinite amount of time, with the block being unable to be removed via anomalous means. Blocked profiles will undergo a major digital shift, with the username being crossed out and replaced with the word "EXECUTED", their info page will be changed to a notice saying "This user was a menace to society, he has been publicly executed" among others. Attempts to combat '''Public Execution''' are futile as of now. Protecting pages in an attempt to prevent '''Public Execution''' from manifesting has failed and finding the source code for '''Public Execution''' or the main template page yields no results. Once one deletes an instance of '''Public Execution''', there is an initial 10% chance that they will become a target for '''Public Execution''' on any of their pages, with the odds increasing approximately 5% more per notice deleted. As such, it is hazardous to remove a '''Public Execution''' instance, as that runs the risk of one not managing to stop one in time and being killed by an instance. Many theories have been curated about the reason for '''Public Execution's''' existence. One of the most prominent theories is that '''Public Execution''' was once a virus developed by [[I.R.I.S]] with the goal of potentially launching targeted attacks on the Database's pages. This theory proposes that the virus spiraled out of I.R.I.S's control and developed anomalous properties and its very own sentience which slowly improved with time. However, the theory has no factual connotations backing it and I.R.I.S themselves has denied creating anything similar to '''Public Execution'''. Besides the I.R.I.S theory, other theories have also been proposed, from '''Public Execution''' being the result of a secret project to create an A.I moderator that ended in disaster, to '''Public Execution''' being as a result of a powerful entity or human merging with BACK-NET, though all of these claims are still yet to be proven. {{Underline|FFFFFF}}
We were unintentionally killing others because of our laziness.

I have no idea how we can apologize or make up for the number of victims killed by Public Execution, nor any idea for how we are ever going to instill trust in the Database again.

As of now...

...I'm sorry.
